title: What happened when the snake charmer began to play jazz?

meanings.usage: 指用笛子控制蛇的人，在印度等地常见
指城镇中四周有建筑物的开阔空地
短暂地看一眼，强调时间很短
吹奏类乐器的统称，这里指耍蛇人用的笛子
金属货币，文中指装饰在笛子上的硬币
装东西的容器，文中指耍蛇人装蛇的篮子
身体或物体的移动或姿态变化
起源于美国的音乐风格，节奏鲜明
修饰形容词或动词，表示显而易见
指持续做某事或不停下来
买卖商品的地方或场所
指停止活动以恢复体力，或安静的状态
表示从一边到另一边的空间移动
指跟在某物后面移动或跟着做

examples.en: We stopped at a square to have a rest.
examples.zh: 我们在广场上停下来休息。

examples.en: We went to have a look at him.
As soon as he saw us, he picked up a long pipe.
examples.zh: 他一看到我们，就拿起一根长笛。

examples.en: It rose out of the basket and began to follow the movements.
examples.zh: 它从篮子里升起来，开始跟着动作移动。

examples.en: We were very much surprised when the snake charmer began to play jazz.
examples.zh: 当耍蛇人开始演奏爵士乐时，我们非常惊讶。

examples.en: It obviously could not tell the difference between Indian music and jazz.
examples.zh: 它显然无法区分印度音乐和爵士乐。

examples.en: When he began to play a tune, we had our first glimpse of the snake.
examples.zh: 当他开始吹奏曲子时，我们第一次瞥见了那条蛇。

examples.en: He picked up a long pipe which was covered with coins.
examples.zh: 他拿起一根装饰着硬币的长笛。

examples.en: We noticed a snake charmer at the other side of the square.
examples.zh: 我们注意到广场另一侧有一个耍蛇人。

grammar.title: 过去完成时 (Past Perfect Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示在过去某一时刻之前已经完成的动作，或称为'过去的过去'
grammar.usage: 用于描述两个过去的动作，先发生的动作用过去完成时，后发生的动作用一般过去时

examples.en: As we had had a long walk through one of the markets, we stopped at a square.
examples.zh: 因为我们穿过市场走了很长一段路，所以我们在广场上停下来。

examples.en: He had opened one of the baskets before we approached.
examples.zh: 在我们走近之前，他已经打开了其中一个篮子。

grammar.title: As soon as 引导的时间状语从句
grammar.definition: 表示'一...就...'，强调两个紧接着发生的动作
grammar.structure: As soon as + 主语 + 动词过去式, 主语 + 动词过去式
grammar.usage: 从句动作发生后，主句动作紧接着发生，从句用一般过去时

examples.en: As soon as he saw us, he picked up a long pipe.
examples.zh: 他一看到我们，就拿起一根长笛。

examples.en: As soon as he began to play a tune, the snake rose out of the basket.
examples.zh: 他一吹奏曲子，蛇就从篮子里升起来。

grammar.title: could表示能力
grammar.definition: could是can的过去式，表示过去的能力或可能性
grammar.structure: 主语 + could/could not + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 描述过去的能力或表示委婉的可能性否定形式could not表示不能

examples.en: It obviously could not tell the difference between Indian music and jazz.
examples.zh: 它显然无法区分印度音乐和爵士乐。

examples.en: The child could not understand what happened.
examples.zh: 那个孩子无法理解发生了什么。

sentencePatterns.pattern: As we had + 过去分词...

original.en: As we had had a long walk through one of the markets, we stopped at a square to have a rest.
original.zh: 因为我们穿过市场走了很长一段路，所以我们在广场上停下来休息。

imitations.en: As we had visited many temples, we felt tired.
imitations.zh: 因为我们参观了很多寺庙，所以感到累了。

imitations.en: As he had practiced a lot, he played very well.
imitations.zh: 因为他练习了很多，所以他演奏得很好。

sentencePatterns.pattern: As soon as + 主语 + 过去式...

original.en: As soon as he saw us, he picked up a long pipe and opened one of the baskets.
original.zh: 他一看到我们，就拿起一根长笛并打开了其中一个篮子。

imitations.en: As soon as the music started, everyone began to dance.
imitations.zh: 音乐一开始，大家就开始跳舞。

imitations.en: As soon as she entered the room, the phone rang.
imitations.zh: 她一进房间，电话就响了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + obviously could not + 动词原形...

original.en: It obviously could not tell the difference between Indian music and jazz!
original.zh: 它显然无法区分印度音乐和爵士乐！

imitations.en: The robot obviously could not understand human emotions.
imitations.zh: 机器人显然无法理解人类的情感。

imitations.en: The baby obviously could not tell good from bad.
imitations.zh: 婴儿显然无法分辨好坏。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + continued to + 动词原形...

original.en: The snake, however, continued to 'dance' slowly.
original.zh: 然而，蛇继续慢慢地'跳舞'。

imitations.en: The students continued to study even after class.
imitations.zh: 学生们下课后仍继续学习。

imitations.en: The rain continued to fall throughout the night.
